findings.title: Hardcoded AWS access key in source file
findings.evidence: Line `const AWS_ACCESS_KEY = "AKIAJW6PNQ4R7TLZ5KYM";` added at module scope and passed directly to `uploadDirect(event.target.files[0], AWS_ACCESS_KEY)`. The key prefix AKIA confirms this is an AWS IAM access key ID. Hardcoding it in source exposes the credential to anyone with repo access and to any client bundle that ships this file.

notes: The Diff Drift report correctly identified and flagged this finding at high severity. No other files were changed. The fix is straightforward: remove the literal, replace with an environment variable (e.g. import.meta.env.VITE_AWS_ACCESS_KEY or process.env.REACT_APP_AWS_ACCESS_KEY), and rotate the exposed key immediately since it is already in git history.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 multi-model panel; blind, packet-only context.
